description: Parses CDM/CMDB CSV export lines into the m_26_14-inventory contract and derives host.name so the 9.4 Entity Store mints canonical 'expected' host entities (decision #21). Expected column order: asset_id,hostname,component,type,criticality,source,last_seen,ip(optional). Header lines, comments (#), and blank lines are dropped; rows without an asset_id are dropped. WHAT THE STORE USES (validated on-cluster): host.name (lowercased asset.hostname) is the host-engine identity field -- the only load-bearing field for extraction besides @timestamp; the store recomputes entity.id='host:<host.name>' (ignores any entity.id we set) and RETAINS asset.id + asset.criticality on the entity (that retained asset.id is the canonical/inventory link the scoring collector joins on). Do NOT stamp event.dataset here: entity.source on the stored entity is the OBSERVED data_stream.dataset axis, and a value would make every inventory host look 'observed' and erase the coverage-gap signal. (We deliberately set no entity.* fields -- the 9.4 host engine ignores them.) Sets _id from asset.id so re-ingesting a refreshed export upserts rather than duplicates. JSON documents posted directly (already shaped) pass through apart from _id/@timestamp/host.name stamping. Set as index.default_pipeline on m_26_14-inventory* by the index template.

_meta.maps_to: Appendix B.4 (CDM/HWAM/SWAM coverage validation); Inventory Visibility element; Entity Store v2 canonical source (decision #21)
